title: xanthophyllum rheophilum
intro: Xanthophyllum rheophilum, also known as the river sandalwood, is a species of flowering plant in the family Polygalaceae. It is native to Borneo and is valued for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antioxidant properties. The plant has been traditionally used to treat various health issues, including fever, rheumatism, and skin conditions, due to its health benefits such as reducing pain and inflammation. The herbal preparations of Xanthophyllum rheophilum typically involve the extraction of its essential oils or the use of its leaves and roots in traditional medicine, often in the form of decoctions or infusions.
